bands.label: Absolute Beginner (0-600)
bands.goal: Know the rules, never hang a piece for free, and deliver the basic checkmates.

units.title: The Board & The Pieces
units.goal: Move every piece legally and understand the special rules.

lessons.title: How the Pieces Move
lessons.summary: Learn the movement of every piece and how captures work on the board.
objectives: Move each piece legally
Capture an enemy piece
Recognise an illegal move

lessons.title: Special Moves
lessons.summary: Castling, en passant, and pawn promotion - the rules beyond the basics.
objectives: Castle on both sides legally
Capture en passant
Promote a pawn to a queen

lessons.title: Check, Checkmate & Stalemate
lessons.summary: Tell apart the three king situations that decide every game.
objectives: Get out of check three different ways
Distinguish checkmate from stalemate
Avoid stalemate when you are winning

lessons.title: Piece Values & Trades
lessons.summary: Count material with the standard point values and avoid bad trades.
objectives: Assign point values to each piece
Tell a good trade from a bad one
Refuse to give up a piece for nothing

units.title: First Checkmates
units.goal: Deliver mate in one and finish with king-and-major-piece against a lone king.

lessons.title: Mate in One
lessons.summary: Spot the single move that ends the game on the spot.
objectives: Find a forced mate in one
Check the king has no escape squares
Confirm the checking piece is defended

lessons.title: Overkill Mates (K+Q, K+2R)
lessons.summary: Use a large material edge to corner and mate a lone king.
objectives: Mate with king and queen
Mate with king and two rooks (the ladder)
Drive the king to the edge without stalemate

lessons.title: King & Rook vs King
lessons.summary: The box and ladder technique to mate with a single rook.
objectives: Shrink the king's box with the rook
Use the king to take the opposition
Deliver the back-rank mate

units.title: Don't Hang Pieces
units.goal: See free captures for both sides and defend what is attacked.

lessons.title: Spot a Free Capture
lessons.summary: Find undefended enemy pieces you can simply take for free.
objectives: Identify an undefended enemy piece
Win a hanging piece in one move
Check your own pieces are not hanging

lessons.title: The Fork (Introduction)
lessons.summary: One piece attacks two targets at once - your first real tactic.
objectives: Attack two pieces with one move
Choose a fork that wins material
Recognise a knight fork pattern

lessons.title: Defend a Hanging Piece
lessons.summary: Save an attacked piece by defending, moving, or counter-attacking.
objectives: Add a defender to an attacked piece
Move an attacked piece to safety
Block or capture the attacker

bands.label: Beginner (600-1000)
bands.goal: Master the core tactical motifs, opening principles, a first opening, and basic king-and-pawn ideas.

units.title: Pins, Skewers & Forks
units.goal: Recognise and execute the four foundational tactical motifs.

lessons.title: The Pin
lessons.summary: A piece cannot move without exposing a more valuable one behind it.
objectives: Pin an enemy piece to its king or queen
Pile up on a pinned piece
Avoid leaving your own pieces pinned

lessons.title: The Skewer
lessons.summary: Attack a valuable piece so that moving it exposes a lesser one behind.
objectives: Skewer a king or queen to win the piece behind
Distinguish a skewer from a pin
Set up a skewer with a check

lessons.title: Knight & Pawn Forks
lessons.summary: Use knight geometry and pawn pushes to attack two pieces at once.
objectives: Win material with a knight fork
Spot the royal fork (king and queen)
Fork two pieces with a pawn

lessons.title: Discovered Attack
lessons.summary: Move one piece to unleash an attack from a long-range piece behind it.
objectives: Uncover an attack by moving a blocker
Combine a discovery with a threat
Recognise a discovered check

units.title: Back-Rank & Mating Nets
units.goal: Deliver the most common short forced mates.

lessons.title: Back-Rank Mate
lessons.summary: Mate a king trapped on its back rank by its own pawns.
objectives: Deliver a back-rank mate
Create luft to avoid being mated
Remove the defender of the back rank

lessons.title: Mate in Two (Introduction)
lessons.summary: Force mate over two moves with a quiet or checking first move.
objectives: Calculate a forced mate in two
Find the key first move
Confirm every black reply is covered

lessons.title: Smothered Mate (Introduction)
lessons.summary: The knight mates a king hemmed in by its own pieces.
objectives: Recognise the smothered-mate pattern
Set up the queen sacrifice that forces it
Deliver mate with the knight

units.title: Opening Principles
units.goal: Open soundly with the center, development, and king safety - then learn a first opening.

lessons.title: Center, Develop, Castle
lessons.summary: The three opening priorities every game starts with.
objectives: Fight for the center with a pawn
Develop knights and bishops quickly
Castle within the first ten moves

lessons.title: The Italian Game
lessons.summary: A classical 1.e4 opening that reaches a sound, active setup.
objectives: Reach a sound Italian setup
Develop the bishop to c4
Understand the key central break d4

lessons.title: The London System
lessons.summary: A solid 1.d4 system with the same setup against almost anything.
objectives: Build the London pawn triangle
Develop the dark-squared bishop outside the chain
Know the standard plan for both sides

units.title: King & Pawn Basics
units.goal: Win and draw the most fundamental pawn endgames.

lessons.title: The Opposition
lessons.summary: Use the opposition to control key squares with your king.
objectives: Take the direct opposition
Outflank the enemy king
Use opposition to escort a pawn

lessons.title: Promoting a Pawn
lessons.summary: Shepherd a passed pawn to the eighth rank and queen it.
objectives: Calculate whether a pawn queens in a race
Use the king to support promotion
Avoid stalemate after queening

bands.label: Intermediate Beginner (1000-1400)
bands.goal: Build combinations, calculate cleanly, master king-and-pawn technique, and learn first strategy.

units.title: Removing Defenders & Combinations
units.goal: Chain motifs together to win material with combinations.

lessons.title: Deflection
lessons.summary: Distract a piece from a defensive duty, then strike.
objectives: Deflect a defender off a key square
Win material after the deflection
Spot overworked defenders

lessons.title: Attraction
lessons.summary: Lure an enemy piece to a square where it can be punished.
objectives: Attract the king into a mating net
Sacrifice to drag a piece to a fork square
Combine attraction with a follow-up tactic

lessons.title: Capturing the Defender
lessons.summary: Remove the piece that guards another, then take the prize.
objectives: Identify the critical defender
Trade or take the defender first
Collect the now-undefended piece

lessons.title: Intermezzo (Zwischenzug)
lessons.summary: Insert a forcing in-between move before the expected reply.
objectives: Find an in-between check or threat
Improve the result by changing move order
Avoid auto-recapturing too soon

units.title: Forced Mates
units.goal: Calculate longer forced mates and sacrificial attacks on the king.

lessons.title: Mate in Two
lessons.summary: Sharpen calculation on clean two-move forced mates.
objectives: Calculate forced mate in two reliably
Eliminate every defensive try
Choose between several mating ideas

lessons.title: Sacrificial Mating Attacks
lessons.summary: Sacrifice material to tear open the king and force mate.
objectives: Sacrifice to expose the enemy king
Bring reserves into the attack
Judge when a sacrifice is sound

units.title: Essential King & Pawn
units.goal: Convert and hold the key pawn endgames with correct technique.

lessons.title: K+P vs K (Key Squares)
lessons.summary: The key-square rule that decides king-and-pawn vs king.
objectives: Find the pawn's key squares
Win with the king in front of the pawn
Hold the draw as the defender

lessons.title: The Outside Passed Pawn
lessons.summary: Use a distant passer to decoy the king and win on the other wing.
objectives: Create an outside passed pawn
Use it to divert the enemy king
Convert the resulting material edge

units.title: First Strategy
units.goal: Make positional decisions about squares, files, and bishops.

lessons.title: Weak Squares & Outposts
lessons.summary: Plant a piece on a protected square the enemy cannot challenge.
objectives: Identify a weak square in the enemy camp
Place a knight on a protected outpost
Trade to keep your outpost knight

lessons.title: Open Files for Rooks
lessons.summary: Seize open and half-open files to activate your rooks.
objectives: Occupy an open file with a rook
Double rooks on a file
Invade the seventh rank

lessons.title: Good vs Bad Bishop
lessons.summary: Recognise when a bishop is hemmed in by its own pawns.
objectives: Tell a good bishop from a bad one
Fix enemy pawns on the bishop's colour
Trade off your bad bishop

bands.label: Intermediate (1400-1800)
bands.goal: Handle advanced motifs, theoretical rook endgames, calculation discipline, and a deeper repertoire.

units.title: Advanced Motifs
units.goal: Add the subtle, harder-to-spot tactical ideas to your toolkit.

lessons.title: Interference
lessons.summary: Block the line between a defender and what it protects.
objectives: Cut a defensive line with an interposing move
Sacrifice to interfere with coordination
Win material once the defence is severed

lessons.title: X-Ray & Battery
lessons.summary: Attack or defend through a piece along a line, and stack pieces on it.
objectives: Set up a battery on an open line
Use an x-ray to win material
Defend a piece through an enemy attacker

lessons.title: The Quiet Move
lessons.summary: A non-checking, non-capturing move that creates a decisive threat.
objectives: Find the quiet move inside a combination
Resist the urge to check or capture
Verify the threat is unstoppable

lessons.title: Overloaded Pieces
lessons.summary: Exploit a piece that is the sole defender of two things at once.
objectives: Spot a piece guarding two targets
Attack one duty to break the other
Convert the resulting win of material

units.title: Theoretical Rook Endgames
units.goal: Know the must-know winning and drawing rook-endgame methods.

lessons.title: The Lucena Position
lessons.summary: Build a bridge to promote a pawn with rook and king.
objectives: Set up the bridge with the rook
Shelter the king from checks
Promote the pawn cleanly

lessons.title: The Philidor Position
lessons.summary: Hold the draw a pawn down with the third-rank defence.
objectives: Set up the third-rank defence
Switch to checking from behind
Avoid losing the drawn position

lessons.title: Rook on the Seventh Rank
lessons.summary: Activate a rook on the seventh to harvest pawns and confine the king.
objectives: Invade with the rook on the seventh
Combine the seventh rank with a passed pawn
Coordinate two rooks on the seventh

units.title: Calculation & Visualization
units.goal: Calculate forcing lines accurately and select candidate moves.

lessons.title: Forcing Moves First
lessons.summary: Look at checks, captures, and threats before anything else.
objectives: List all checks, captures, and threats
Calculate the most forcing line first
Visualise the position several moves ahead

lessons.title: Candidate Moves & Elimination
lessons.summary: Generate candidate moves, then prune to the best one.
objectives: Generate two or three candidate moves
Compare lines and eliminate the worst
Commit to the move you calculated

units.title: Repertoire Deepening
units.goal: Carry a coherent opening repertoire for both colours into the middlegame.

lessons.title: A White Repertoire (1.e4 / 1.d4)
lessons.summary: Pick a main first move and learn the resulting middlegame plans.
objectives: Choose a consistent first move as White
Learn the key tabiyas of your opening
Know the typical middlegame plan

lessons.title: Black Defences vs 1.e4
lessons.summary: Build a reliable answer to the most common White first move.
objectives: Choose a sound defence to 1.e4
Reach a comfortable structure
Avoid early opening traps

lessons.title: Black Defences vs 1.d4
lessons.summary: Round out the repertoire with a system against 1.d4.
objectives: Choose a sound defence to 1.d4
Coordinate it with your anti-e4 setup
Understand the central pawn breaks

bands.label: Advanced (1800-2000)
bands.goal: Apply prophylaxis, deep endgame theory, conversion technique, and long forced sequences.

units.title: Prophylaxis & Maneuvering
units.goal: Anticipate the opponent's plans and improve slowly when no tactic exists.

lessons.title: Prophylactic Thinking
lessons.summary: Ask what the opponent wants and stop it before improving your own position.
objectives: Identify the opponent's intended plan
Play a move that prevents it
Balance prevention with your own ideas

lessons.title: Zugzwang & Triangulation
lessons.summary: Force the opponent to ruin their own position by losing a tempo.
objectives: Recognise a zugzwang position
Triangulate to pass the move
Set up reciprocal zugzwang in the endgame

units.title: Higher Endgame Theory
units.goal: Handle major-piece endgames and convert won positions with technique.

lessons.title: R+2 vs R
lessons.summary: Convert the extra pawns in a double-rook-pawn endgame.
objectives: Coordinate rook and pawns against the lone rook
Avoid the standard drawing fortresses
Make progress without blundering a pawn

lessons.title: Queen Endgames
lessons.summary: Use checks, the king's shelter, and passed pawns in queen endings.
objectives: Shelter the king from perpetual checks
Escort a passed pawn with the queen
Judge perpetual-check draws

lessons.title: Conversion Technique
lessons.summary: Win won positions: simplify, avoid counterplay, and convert cleanly.
objectives: Trade into a clearly winning endgame
Shut down enemy counterplay
Convert a decisive advantage without rushing

units.title: Long & Very-Long Combinations
units.goal: Calculate deep forced mates and lengthy forcing sequences.

lessons.title: Mate in Four to Five
lessons.summary: Calculate deeper forced mates without losing the thread.
objectives: Calculate a forced mate in four or five
Track the king through every escape
Choose the most forcing move order

lessons.title: Very-Long Forced Lines
lessons.summary: Hold an extended forcing sequence accurately to the end.
objectives: Calculate a long forced line to its conclusion
Keep the evaluation in mind at the end
Spot the resource that holds it together

units.title: The Attacking Game
units.goal: Conduct dynamic attacks and sound sacrifices for the initiative.

lessons.title: Opposite-Side Castling Races
lessons.summary: Storm pawns toward the enemy king when kings sit on opposite wings.
objectives: Launch a pawn storm against the castled king
Open files faster than the opponent
Judge who is winning the race

lessons.title: Sacrifices for the Initiative
lessons.summary: Give up material for lasting pressure rather than a forced win.
objectives: Sacrifice for long-term initiative
Keep the opponent on the defensive
Judge compensation without a forced line
